title: Market Brief — Tuesday, May 5, 2026
summary: GPU supply is bifurcated: A100s sit at 85% availability (oversupply), H100s at 70%, and MI300X at 60%, while B200s are tight at 25% and GB200s face severe shortage at 10% with no tradeable price. B200 utilization surged 23.5pp in 30 days to 92.2%, the biggest move on the board, signaling frontier training demand is absorbing next-gen silicon faster than supply can scale.
paragraphs: GPU supply is bifurcated: A100s sit at 85% availability (oversupply), H100s at 70%, and MI300X at 60%, while B200s are tight at 25% and GB200s face severe shortage at 10% with no tradeable price. B200 utilization surged 23.5pp in 30 days to 92.2%, the biggest move on the board, signaling frontier training demand is absorbing next-gen silicon faster than supply can scale.

At TCO-adjusted cost (1.25x spot), running Llama 70B on H100s costs roughly $9.84/M tokens, meaning DeepSeek V3.2 at $0.378/M output is selling at a loss of approximately $9.46 per million tokens — burning cash at industrial scale. DeepSeek: Burning Cash. OpenAI's o3 Pro at $80/M output prints money on any hardware; Meta's Llama 4 Maverick at $0.60/M is also deep underwater. OpenAI premium tier: Strong. Google: Neutral. Anthropic: Neutral.

B200 spot at $4.20/hr with only 25% availability is undervalued relative to its utilization surge — lock in forward capacity now before the next squeeze. A100s at 85% availability and flat utilization are oversupplied legacy iron; sell or redeploy any uncommitted A100 clusters.
bullets: 25% availability on B200s puts next-gen compute one demand spike from severe shortage.
10% GB200 availability signals the most acute scarcity in the entire GPU stack.
$4.04/hr OCPI on H200s carries 86.6% annualized volatility, the highest of any GPU class.
97.6% H100 utilization is the board's peak, up 8.8pp in 30 days despite 70% nominal availability.
$0.378/M output from DeepSeek V3.2 sits roughly 96% below TCO-adjusted inference cost.
21.41 on the LLMflation Index means frontier token prices have fallen nearly 79% since GPT-4 launch.
